info.name: Abroad Matrimony API
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for the Abroad Matrimony backend.

Organized by domain/feature group. Use the **Abroad Matrimony — Local** environment for local development.

## How to use
1. Import this file into Postman.
2. Import `postman-environment.json` and select it as the active environment.
3. Run **Admin — Auth / Admin Login** first to populate `{{admin_token}}`.
4. Run **Auth / Request OTP** → **Auth / Verify OTP** to populate `{{access_token}}`.

## Maintenance
See `docs/api/STANDARDS.md` for rules on keeping this file in sync with the codebase.

variable.description: User JWT access token — populated after OTP verify
Admin JWT access token — populated after admin login

item.name: Health
item.description: API health check — no auth required.

request.description: Returns 200 with service uptime and version. Use this to verify the gateway is reachable.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Phone OTP authentication, JWT token management, Firebase token, trusted device bypass.

request.description:
Sends a 6-digit OTP to the provided phone number via Twilio Verify. Rate-limited to 5 requests per phone per 15 minutes.

Phone must be in E.164 format (e.g. +919876543210).

Verifies the 6-digit OTP. Creates the user row on first login (upsert). Issues an access token (15 min) + refresh token (30 days).

The test script automatically saves the access token as `{{access_token}}`.

`fingerprint` must be a UUID v4 that uniquely identifies the device (max 5 devices per user).
Rotates the refresh token. Old token is revoked immediately. Reuse of a consumed token triggers all-device logout (security).

Skips OTP for devices that have successfully completed OTP within the trust window (default 90 days). Returns 401 if device is not trusted — Flutter should fall back to the full OTP flow. Rate-limited to 10 attempts per phone per hour.
Revokes the refresh token for the current device session. Access token remains valid until its 15-minute TTL expires.
Revokes all refresh tokens for the authenticated user across all devices.
Returns a short-lived Firebase custom auth token. Flutter uses this to sign in to Firebase SDK before subscribing to Firestore real-time listeners. Token is scoped to the authenticated user's UID.

item.name: Profile
item.description: User profile creation and management — real-life answers, story prompts, photo upload.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Arjun Sharma",
  "dateOfBirth": "1992-05-15",
  "gender": "MALE",
  "currentCity": "London",
  "currentCountry": "GB",
  "settlementIntent": "OPEN_TO_BOTH",
  "bio": "Software engineer who loves hiking and cooking."
}

request.description: Creates the user's profile. One profile per user (subsequent calls return 409). `gender` must be one of: MALE, FEMALE, NON_BINARY, PREFER_NOT_TO_SAY. `settlementIntent`: RETURN_HOME, STAY_ABROAD, OPEN_TO_BOTH.
Returns the full profile for the authenticated user including real-life answers, story prompts, photos, and completion score.

variable.description: One of: CHILDREN, FAITH, DIET, MONEY, CAREER, LOCATION, FAMILY, LIFESTYLE, PARENTING, PETS, POLITICS, LANGUAGES

request.description: Creates or updates a single real-life answer. `questionKey` values: CHILDREN, FAITH, DIET, MONEY, CAREER, LOCATION, FAMILY, LIFESTYLE, PARENTING, PETS, POLITICS, LANGUAGES. Answer is free text (max 500 chars).
Creates or updates a story prompt answer. `promptKey` values: MOST_SPONTANEOUS, DINNER_GUEST, PERFECT_SUNDAY. Answer is free text (max 1000 chars).

formdata.description: JPEG, PNG, or WebP — max 5 MB

request.description: Uploads a profile photo to S3 (private bucket, served via CloudFront). Max 6 photos per user. Accepted types: JPEG, PNG, WebP. Max size: 5 MB. Returns the CDN URL of the uploaded photo.

variable.description: UUID of the user whose profile to fetch

request.description: Returns the public profile view for any user ID. Includes completion score, real-life answers, story prompts, and photos. Returns 404 if the profile does not exist.

item.name: Discover
item.description: Paginated discovery feed — sorted by compatibility score.

query.description: Opaque cursor from previous page's meta.nextCursor — omit for first page

request.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of compatible users sorted by compatibility score descending. Users that have been previously seen are excluded. Respects the `ALGO_V2` feature flag (if enabled, uses the v2 scoring algorithm).

item.name: Conversations
item.description: Messaging conversations and message history. Messages are stored in Firebase Firestore for real-time delivery; these REST endpoints provide fallback access and mutation.

request.description: Returns all conversations for the authenticated user, sorted by most recent message. Includes the other participant's name + avatar and the last message preview.
Returns details for a single conversation. Returns 403 if the authenticated user is not a participant.

query.description: Opaque cursor for older messages — omit for most recent page

request.description: Returns cursor-paginated message history for a conversation, newest-first. Pass `cursor` from `meta.nextCursor` to page backwards through history.

Sends a message to the conversation. `type` values: TEXT, IMAGE, VOICE, SYSTEM.

- TEXT: `content` required.
- IMAGE: `content` is the S3/CloudFront URL (obtain from upload-url endpoint).
- VOICE: `content` is the S3/CloudFront URL, `durationSeconds` required (1–600).

If the recipient is offline, an FCM push notification is sent automatically.

query.description: MIME type: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, audio/mp4, audio/mpeg

request.description: Returns a short-lived S3 pre-signed upload URL. Flutter uploads the media file directly to S3 using this URL, then passes the resulting CDN URL as the `content` field in Send Message.

REST fallback for read receipts. Marks all messages in the conversation as read for the authenticated user. Firestore real-time listeners on Flutter handle this natively; this endpoint is for offline / background sync.

item.name: Messages
item.description: Message-level actions (flagging, moderation).

request.description: Reports a message for moderation. `reason` values: SPAM, HARASSMENT, INAPPROPRIATE_CONTENT, FAKE_PROFILE, SCAM, OTHER. `description` is optional free text (max 500 chars). A message is automatically hidden in Firestore when it reaches 3 flags.

item.name: Payment — Stripe
item.description: Stripe Checkout sessions and webhook handler.

request.description: Creates a Stripe Checkout session for the Founding Member plan. Returns a `checkoutUrl` — redirect the user's browser to this URL. On success, Stripe fires `checkout.session.completed` to the webhook, which activates the membership.

header.description: Generated by Stripe — do not set manually in real use. Use Stripe CLI for local testing: stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/v1/payment/stripe/webhook

request.description: Stripe webhook endpoint. Raw body (not JSON-parsed) is required for signature verification. Do not test manually — use the Stripe CLI: `stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/v1/payment/stripe/webhook`. Handles: checkout.session.completed, invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.deleted, charge.refunded.

item.name: Payment — Razorpay
item.description: Razorpay order creation, payment capture, and webhook handler.

request.description: Creates a Razorpay order for the Founding Member plan. Returns `orderId`, `amount` (paise), `currency`. Flutter uses these fields to open the Razorpay checkout SDK.

Verifies the Razorpay payment signature and captures the payment. `signature` = HMAC-SHA256(`orderId|paymentId`, Razorpay key secret) — generated by the Razorpay SDK on the Flutter side. Activates the membership on success.

header.description: HMAC-SHA256 of raw body using Razorpay webhook secret — set by Razorpay automatically

request.description: Razorpay webhook endpoint. Signature is verified against the raw request body. Handles: payment.captured, payment.failed, refund.created.

item.name: Payment — Membership
item.description: Founding Member membership status.

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's active membership record, or 404 if no active membership exists. Includes plan, status, provider, amount, and expiry date.

item.name: Payment — Diamonds
item.description: Diamond credit balance, purchase, and spending.

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's current diamond balance. Balance is read from the latest `balanceAfter` entry in the append-only diamond ledger.
Creates a Stripe Checkout session to purchase a diamond package. Returns a `checkoutUrl`. After successful payment, Stripe fires the webhook which credits the diamonds via the ledger.

Deducts diamonds from the user's balance. Returns 409 if the balance is insufficient. `reason` values: BOOST_PROFILE, SUPER_LIKE, UNLOCK_CONTACT, GIFT, OTHER. All ledger entries are append-only — no balance updates, only new rows with `delta` and `balanceAfter`.

item.name: Admin — Auth
item.description: Admin authentication — separate from user auth. Admin tokens use a different JWT secret.

request.description:
Admin login with email + bcrypt password + TOTP code (Google Authenticator / Authy). Returns an admin JWT access token. The test script saves this as `{{admin_token}}`.

Note: Admin API routes are under `/admin/` (not `/api/v1/`).

item.name: Admin — Moderation
item.description: Flag review queue and resolution. Requires MODERATOR admin role or higher.

query.description: Filter by status: PENDING, REVIEWED, DISMISSED

request.description: Returns all moderation flags raised against a specific user's messages. Requires MODERATOR admin role.
Resolves or dismisses a moderation flag. `action` values: DISMISS, WARN_USER, HIDE_MESSAGE, SUSPEND_USER, BAN_USER. `note` is optional free text for the audit log. Requires MODERATOR admin role.

item.name: Admin — Payment
item.description: Admin payment management. Requires SUPERADMIN role.

request.description: Processes a refund for a payment. Marks the payment record as refunded and inserts a reversal entry into the diamond ledger if `reverseDiamonds` is true. Does NOT automatically trigger the refund at Stripe/Razorpay — that must be initiated in their dashboards first. Requires SUPERADMIN admin role.

item.name: Auth — Email
item.description: Email magic link authentication (Continue with email flow).

request.description: Send a one-time magic link to the provided email. Rate-limited: 3/hour/email.

exec: const r = pm.response.json(); if (r.success && r.data.tokens) { pm.environment.set('access_token', r.data.tokens.accessToken); pm.environment.set('user_id', r.data.userId); }

request.description: Exchange magic-link token for JWT pair. Auto-saves access_token and user_id.

item.name: Profile — Extensions
item.description: Voice intro upload and profile pause/resume.

request.description: Upload a voice introduction to S3. One per user — replaces existing.
Pause (or resume) profile visibility from discovery and intro drops.

item.name: Connections
item.description: Send, accept, pass, and list connection requests.

item.name: Groups
item.description: Regional community circles — browse, join, leave.

request.description: Deducts creditCost credits if CREDIT_GATED. Max 2 groups per user.

item.name: Verification
item.description: Identity document and selfie submission.

item.name: Introductions
item.description: Weekly curated intro drop — this week's pool, history, connect, pass.

request.description: Spends creditCost credits and sends a connection request.

item.name: Gatherings
item.description: Community events — browse, RSVP, attendees.

item.name: Weekly Prompts
item.description: Weekly community prompt — respond, browse, resonate.

item.name: Saved Profiles
item.description: Save, label, note, and remove saved profiles.

item.name: Signals
item.description: Weekly activity signals dashboard.

request.description: Returns profile views, connection requests, resonates, intro pool size, action queue, and momentum score. Redis-cached 15 min.

item.name: Trust Center
item.description: Trust score, layers, block, unblock, report.

item.name: Habits
item.description: Daily habit logging, streaks, weekly reflection, Sunday check-in.

request.description: Idempotent daily log. Habit keys: MORNING_ROUTINE, EXERCISE, HEALTHY_EATING, MINDFULNESS, JOURNALING, SOCIAL_CONNECTION, LEARNING

item.name: Match Tuning
item.description: Personal match preference tuning.

request.description: Partial update. Triggers async rescore via BullMQ.

item.name: Payment — Credits
item.description: Credit balance, spend, purchase, and transaction history.

request.description: Append-only credit ledger. Newest first.

item.name: Admin — Users
item.description: User search, detail, role management, suspension.

item.name: Admin — Analytics
item.description: Platform-level engagement and signup metrics.

item.name: Admin — Verification
item.description: ID document and selfie review queue.
